title: Thelypteris polyotica
intro: Thelypteris polyotica, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris polyotica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris polyotica is often used to make teas, infusions, and infusates, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris polyotica is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae and the order Polypodiales.
